title: cerinthe maculata subsp. auriculata
intro: Cerinthe maculata subsp. auriculata, also known as Dyer's Greenweed, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerinthe maculata subsp. auriculata are attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
